posts.facebook:
Paystack vs Stripe for South African products:

Stripe works. Paystack wins on local card success rates, bank transfer support, and ZAR settlement without currency conversion friction.

For SA-first products, Paystack is the default. Not a preference, a practical choice.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Paystack vs Stripe in South Africa. The practical answer. #Paystack #Fintech #AfricanTech #SouthAfrica

posts.facebook:
My first step on every project is not code. It's a PRD.

Writing forces the clarity that conversation never does. Before I touch a database schema or a UI, we have a document that answers: What are we building? Who for? What is the smallest version that proves the idea?

Most junior devs skip this. That's why you end up 3 months in with something that solves the wrong problem.

instagram.caption: Documentation first. Code second. Always. #ProductEngineering #MVP #TechFounder #BuildInPublic

posts.facebook:
The most expensive line in any software project is not the dev rate.

It is the requirement that changes after the data model is built.

A schema change mid-build can cost more in rework than the original sprint. This is why I lock data contracts before writing a single component.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: The most expensive thing in software isn't the dev rate. It's the requirement that changes too late. #Engineering #TechFounder

posts.facebook:
Load shedding is not an excuse. It is a product requirement.

SA-built software needs offline queuing, graceful degradation, and retry logic. If your app just freezes during a power cut, that is a design failure.

Building for African realities means building for this.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Load shedding is a product requirement, not an excuse. Build for African realities. #LoadShedding #AfricanTech #BuildForAfrica

posts.facebook:
POPIA is not optional.

If your South African product collects names, emails, IDs, or health data, you are bound by the Protection of Personal Information Act.

Most junior-built products have no data retention policy, no deletion flow, and no audit trail. That is a regulatory liability that grows with every new user.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: POPIA compliance is not a nice-to-have. It is a legal requirement for SA products. #POPIA #DataPrivacy #SouthAfrica

Vercel, Railway, and GCP: how I choose.

Vercel for frontend and serverless APIs. Railway when you need persistent processes and a simple ops model. GCP when data sovereignty, compliance, or scale demand it.

Most South African products start on Vercel and Railway. That is not a compromise. It is the right choice for the stage.

SaaS pricing models I have built in the last 3 years:

- Per seat (user count)
- Per transaction (usage-based)
- Per feature tier (freemium gating)
- Per usage unit (API calls, records)

Each model creates different behaviour from customers. Choose based on how your customers get value, not what is easiest to implement.

Mobile data costs in South Africa are still a real constraint.

An app that loads 4MB of images on the home screen, makes 12 API calls before displaying content, and has no skeleton state is not just slow. It actively excludes users.

Performance is a product decision, not a technical detail.

posts.facebook:
The three questions that determine your app's budget:

1. Does it need payments?
2. Does it need real-time features (chat, location, live updates)?
3. Does it need admin tools, or will staff use the same app as customers?

Each yes adds scope. Knowing this before the first call makes the conversation faster for everyone.

qwabi.co.za/app-development-cost-south-africa

instagram.caption: Three questions that determine your app's budget. Know the answers before your first dev call. #AppDev #Budget #StartupAdvice

posts.facebook:
The difference between an MVP and a prototype:

A prototype is a simulation. An MVP is production-grade software with limited scope.

If real users are using it with real data and real payments, it is an MVP. If it is a demo for investors with fake data, it is a prototype.

Both have value. They cost different amounts to build correctly.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: MVP vs prototype: not the same thing. Both have value. Different costs. #MVP #Prototype #StartupAdvice

posts.facebook:
RAG (Retrieval-Augmented Generation) is the pattern behind most useful AI products in 2026.

Instead of asking an LLM to remember your documents, you retrieve the relevant chunks at query time and include them in the prompt.

This is how you build AI features that are accurate on your specific domain.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: RAG is the pattern behind most useful AI products right now. Here's the core idea. #RAG #AI #LLM #AIEngineering
instagram.query: rag retrieval augmented generation ai diagram

AI agents in 2026 are most useful when they are narrow and supervised.

An agent that autonomously closes support tickets without human review: high risk, medium value.

Narrow and supervised first.

Education technology in South Africa has a specific challenge: connectivity.

An edutech platform that requires a 4G connection for every lesson delivery will fail in rural and township contexts.

posts.facebook:
Microservices are usually the wrong choice for an early-stage product.

Monolith first. Extract services when you have a clear performance bottleneck or team boundary that justifies the operational complexity.

Most South African startups that chose microservices from day one regretted it before year two.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Microservices from day one is usually a mistake. Monolith first, extract when justified. #Microservices #SoftwareArchitecture #StartupAdvice

posts.facebook:
GraphQL vs REST for South African product APIs:

REST is simpler to build, cache, and maintain for most product categories. GraphQL wins when you have many client types consuming the same data with different shape requirements.

Do not choose GraphQL because it sounds modern. Choose it when the problem demands it.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: GraphQL vs REST. Don't choose GraphQL because it sounds modern. Choose it when the problem demands it. #GraphQL #REST #APIDesign
instagram.query: graphql rest api comparison web development

posts.facebook:
The most valuable hour you can spend before scoping a software project:

Draw the user journey for your most important customer action on a piece of paper.

Every step, every decision point, every place where they could fail or give up.

That drawing will surface more requirements than an hour of conversation.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Before scoping: draw the user journey on paper. It surfaces more requirements than any conversation. #UX #ProductScoping #TechFounder
instagram.query: user journey map drawing flow diagram whiteboard

Offline-capable mobile apps are not a luxury in South Africa.

For field service workers, healthcare workers in rural clinics, and delivery drivers in low-coverage areas: offline mode is the product.

If the app becomes useless when the connection drops, you have not built for the market.

qwabi.co.za

instagram.caption: Offline-capable mobile apps are not a luxury in SA. For field workers and rural clinics, offline mode is the product. #OfflineFirst #AfricanTech #MobileApp
